skill.name: lark-workflow-meeting-summary
skill.description: Summarizes Lark meeting notes across a selected date range and formats them as a structured meeting report.
skill.summary: Create structured summaries from Lark meeting records and meeting note links.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell, filesystem, weak cryptography, and entropy indicators in SKILL.md. Review found the shell findings are authenticated Lark CLI workflow commands, while path traversal, cryptography, and entropy alerts are mostly Markdown and language-content false positives. The skill is publishable with a warning because it can query sensitive meeting metadata and optionally create or update Lark documents through the user's authorized account.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Lark CLI Access to Meeting Data
medium_findings.description: The workflow instructs the assistant to run lark-cli commands for authentication, meeting search, note lookup, document metadata lookup, and optional document creation or update. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but it can expose sensitive meeting titles, note links, transcript links, and generated summaries through the user's authorized Lark account.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and directly operate on Lark meeting and document resources. The behavior appears legitimate for the workflow, but the data sensitivity is clear.

low_findings.title: Relative Sibling Skill References
low_findings.description: The filesystem path traversal alerts are Markdown links to sibling Lark skill documents, not dynamic path traversal logic. They still expand the context the assistant may read, so publishers should ensure those referenced skills are reviewed together.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced paths are visible Markdown links to fixed sibling files. I found no evidence of user-controlled file path construction.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography and Entropy Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert points to the YAML description, and the entropy alert points to the Markdown file start. No cryptographic algorithm, encoded payload, binary data, or hidden execution content was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows front matter and Chinese descriptive text only. There is no evidence of cryptographic code or obfuscated payloads.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on lark-cli and a system date command to query, transform, and publish meeting summary information. This is expected behavior, but users should approve commands and understand the permissions granted to the Lark CLI session.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly requires external commands. The command intent matches the product functionality, so the concern is permission and data handling rather than malicious execution.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time collecting meeting notes across many Lark sessions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to gather Lark meeting records and produce a structured report.
actual_capabilities: Determines a date range for daily, weekly, or custom meeting reviews.
Uses Lark CLI meeting search to collect meeting records in pages.
Retrieves meeting note and transcript document tokens for found meetings.
Looks up document metadata and links for meeting notes and transcripts.
Formats results as a daily overview or weekly meeting report.
Can create or append a Lark document when the user requests it.
limitations: Requires an installed and authorized lark-cli user session.
Works only with Lark resources the current user can access.
Long date ranges must be split into one-month search windows.
Does not summarize unavailable meeting notes beyond marking them missing.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Weekly Meeting Report
use_cases.description: Collect meetings from the past week and turn them into a structured summary with note links.

use_cases.title: Review Daily Meeting Activity
use_cases.description: List today's meetings with topics, available notes, and transcript links.

use_cases.title: Create a Shared Meeting Archive
use_cases.description: Generate a Lark document that collects meeting summaries for a selected project period.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Today
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize my Lark meetings from today. Include meeting titles, times, note links, and transcript links when available.

prompt_templates.title: Create Weekly Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a weekly Lark meeting report for this week. Include overview counts, meetings without notes, and links to notes or transcripts.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Custom Period
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize Lark meetings from June 1 to June 15. Group the results by day and highlight recurring topics.

prompt_templates.title: Publish to Lark Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Lark meeting summary for the past seven days, then create a Lark document with the final report after I approve it.

output_examples.output: Today's meeting overview with total meetings, meetings with notes, meetings without notes, and a per-meeting list of titles, times, note links, and transcript links.

output_examples.input: Create a weekly report for the last seven days.
output_examples.output: A weekly meeting report with summary statistics, daily sections, meeting details, missing-note markers, and linked source documents.

output_examples.input: Publish the approved summary to a Lark document.
output_examples.output: A confirmation-ready report title and document body that can be created or appended in Lark Docs after user approval.

best_practices: Confirm the requested date range before running Lark CLI searches.
Review generated summaries before creating or updating shared documents.
Use the smallest Lark authorization scope that supports the requested task.
anti_patterns: Do not run document creation commands before the user approves the report.
Do not request broader Lark access than the meeting summary task needs.
Do not assume unavailable notes contain content; mark them as unavailable.

faq.question: Does this skill require Lark CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on lark-cli and an authorized user session.

faq.question: Can it summarize meetings from any Lark workspace?
faq.answer: No. It can only access meetings available to the authorized user.

faq.question: Can it create a Lark document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create or append a document when the user requests that action.

faq.question: Does it read full meeting notes?
faq.answer: It retrieves meeting note metadata and links. Full content access depends on the related Lark document permissions.

faq.question: How does it handle meetings without notes?
faq.answer: It marks those meetings as having no available notes in the final report.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium warning. It uses authenticated commands that can access sensitive meeting information.
